item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i forgive you, dado.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Horror based on addiction and loss of control always hits me pretty deep, and the described effects of both withdrawal and overdose on the drug felt unique and disturbing (I especially liked the fact that the overdosers float in midair and eventually vanish into nothing — it fit nicely with the angel imagery and raised some disturbing questions about what exactly the drug was capable of.) The event log and final note were also a great way to close out the article; contagion-style outbreaks that result from tiny slip-ups are always pretty terrifying, and the last line, on top of being fantastically punchy and effective, did a great job of tying the skip back to the angel imagery and leaving the reader (or at least me) with some pretty chilling existential implications. I can definitely see people objecting to dado being the one who manufactured the drug, but I think it lends the article a nice sense of interconnectivity with the rest of the SCP universe, and also adds a good amount of depth to his character that I find very welcome. Overall, a very enjoyable read, and a solid, well-written article. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The new headers remind me of the "overdecorated goldbar" from the old Wesnoth art forums:

https://www.wesnoth.org/forum/viewtopic.php?t=14281&start=55

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is deeply unsettling. I love it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: there are many points where "clinical language" and "economy of words and descriptions in order to drive home a story" conflict, and this is one of them. ultimately, the exact chemical composition of a hypothetical paradrug has no real narrative significance in comparison to the main arc of Repentant Dado and Holy Shit People Turn Into Angels

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What the——?

Well, this is one dado stuff that is his most terrifying (not that some of dado's other creations have not been terrifying, like his 'solution' to bad eyesight). Relieved to see that it was a mistake, because even dado would know when he has majorly screwed up.

So, is this sorta what happens to people who know too much of SCP-370 ("A Key")? Is this Heaven (whatever religion it may be)? If so…

Don't go heaven trippin', kids.

`

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was thinking that we use the structure of morphine,add a few functional groups and blur out the part that contributes to its anomalous properties(maybe it contains elements from the Foundation's extended periodic table). But even if we don't add the structural formula, It would still make sense(from an in-universe perspective) for the Foundation to determine its physical properties. It would make it more clinical,I guess.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Frankly, those details are irrelevant to the article itself and they would also destroy the suspension of disbelief for any chemistry literate readers. Obviously a chemical that we know the structure of would have an IUPAC name and there's a decent chance we have an inkling of what it would do. This thing is an anomaly, so giving the structure of it would clash with that fact hard enough to potentially derail the whole article in an irrelevant way. Better off just not doing that for the sake of the narrative.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dust of angel? I assume that's based on angel dust,the street name for phenylcyclidine(a hallucinogen). When I read the name,I thought it would cause visions and stuff, but it's more like an opioid, I guess. I think it would be great if you added some physical and chemical properties like density,solubility,reactions and maybe even a structural formula

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCPs don't always connect with each other canonically.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
lmao dado what the fuck is wrong with you, crazy ass

1+

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: is this predicated on "all dogs go to heaven" ?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: is dado still active, then? i thought the borger and france fry incident neutralized everything… i still love the man, so +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love skips that are connected to dado. The ending was pretty interesting and I loved the concept behind the entire article, good work! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aww, poor dado. He just wanted to give people good medicine.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is quite good. While the dado text convo was a nice emotion-evoker to bring in, this article really makes its hay when you came back and got into Area-4.

I also hate when people shy away from ending with a pow/punch line. You walked the brave man road and I am here for it. Easy +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I enjoyed the article proper, but holy shit, the damn headers take up a full phone screen and a half before I can even get to the first bit of text. That's frankly beyond obnoxious for such a short article, especially given more than half of the traffic on this site comes from mobile users.

E: Just saw that the Overseer heading is gone. That's a drastic improvement.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: God reading this made me feel viscerally uncomfortable. I already dislike reading about drug use, legal or not, and this got to me by focusing on its addictiveness and ease of spread.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I saw dado mentioned and thought “this will be funny”

But instead it’s fairly haunting, and weirdly I feeL bad for dado in this.

+1 for creepy angel overdoses

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The call log seems like it would be more suited to italics. Other than that, +1.

item.title: angels
item.content:encoded:
I tried this article out a few months ago and it didn't really vibe the way I wanted it to. Cleaned up some of the language and streamlined the intent, and I feel a lot better about it now. dado is a funny talented weird dude, but he's not immune to mistakes. When you're in the business he's in, those mistakes can have shitty consequences.

Anyway, thanks to kirby, rounder, hippo?, nico, and others who checked this out and convinced me it was worth looking at again. I hope you like it!
